title: Digitacalia chiapensis
intro: Digitacalia chiapensis, also known as Chiapas Digitacalia, is a plant species within the Asteraceae family, native to the mountainous regions of Mexico and Guatemala. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as potential antioxidant and analgesic effects. The bioactive constituents of Digitacalia chiapensis have been identified as sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Digitacalia chiapensis is typically prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat conditions such as fever, sore throats, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Digitacalia chiapensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the genus Digitacalia, which is a group of plants closely related to the genus Vernonia. Within the Asteraceae family, Digitacalia chiapensis is part of the subfamily Vernonioideae. The exact classification of Digitacalia chiapensis may be subject to change as new research becomes available. Further study is needed to confirm its precise taxonomic position.
